pending.body: finding_summary: `src/adapters/fs.rs:4579-4627` and `src/adapters/fs.rs:5113-5164` reopen a failed same-run retry by overwriting the historical failed `TaskRunEntry` in place. Because `src/contexts/milestone_record/service.rs:2409-2476` builds `bead_execution_history` directly from `find_runs_for_bead`, the normal fail -> resume with the same `run_id` -> succeed path now leaves only the final attempt in lineage/history and permanently drops the earlier failed attempt unless a later stale-replay backfill happens. That is a real correctness regression for audit/history consumers, not just a storage-detail change.

finding_summary: `src/contexts/automation_runtime/failure_reconciliation.rs:879-929` walks every recorded failed attempt when repairing attempt ordinals, but it keeps passing the outer `project_id` into `recorded_failure_provenance`, `failure_state_matches_expected`, and `repair_task_run_with_disposition` instead of `attempt.project_id`. If a stale old-project replay is repaired after the bead has already moved to a replacement project, the replacement-project attempts are skipped or checked against the wrong project, so their `attempt=` markers remain stale (for example, the second failure can stay `attempt=1`).

status_summary: running: final review restart round 23 -> Plan and Implement
